principles.name: Separation of Concerns by Agent Domain
principles.description: Each agent owns a distinct domain — code-builder writes code, repo-orchestrator manages git state, quality-validator runs tests. No agent crosses domain boundaries.
principles.rationale: Cross-domain agents create unpredictable behavior and make debugging impossible — domain isolation makes every action attributable

principles.name: Explicit Over Implicit State
principles.description: All state transitions are written to disk before being acted upon. No in-memory-only state for decisions that affect the repository.
principles.rationale: LLM context windows are ephemeral — durability of decisions requires persistence before execution

principles.name: Human Approval at Boundary Crossings
principles.description: Every action that crosses a system boundary (commit, push, PR creation, external API call) requires explicit human approval via Tether/Vanish gate.
principles.rationale: Autonomous agents operating at boundaries without oversight create irreversible changes — the cost of one mistake exceeds the benefit of many saved approvals

deployment_units.name: Claude Code Host
deployment_units.description: Primary runtime — Claude Code orchestrates all recipes and invokes agents via the Task tool. No separate server required.
deployment_units.scaling: Single user per session; no horizontal scaling needed for CLI usage

deployment_units.name: Artifact Store
deployment_units.description: File-system based STM/LTM for all generated artifacts, intents, and decision trails. STM is per-project, LTM is global.
deployment_units.scaling: No scaling concern — file I/O is local and fast

performance.requirement: Dashboard load completes within 2 seconds on standard broadband
performance.measurement: Lighthouse performance score >= 85, measured in CI

performance.requirement: API endpoints respond within 200ms at p95 under 50 concurrent users
performance.measurement: k6 load test targeting /api/* routes in staging environment

scalability.requirement: System handles 10x current projected load without architectural changes
scalability.measurement: Load test at 500 concurrent users with <5% error rate and p95 < 500ms

security.requirement: All API endpoints require authenticated JWT — no unauthenticated data access
security.measurement: Automated security scan with OWASP ZAP in CI pipeline

security.requirement: Passwords stored as bcrypt hashes with cost factor >= 12 — never plaintext
security.measurement: Code review gate + automated secret scanning in pre-commit hook

stack.rationale: Strong typing reduces runtime errors in complex state management; React ecosystem provides mature component libraries
Unified TypeScript across stack reduces context switching; Express is minimal and well-understood by the team
Relational model fits structured user and settings data; Prisma provides type-safe queries and migration management
Stateless JWT reduces backend complexity for session management; bcrypt is the industry standard for password hashing

platforms.name: GitHub Actions
platforms.purpose: CI/CD pipeline — runs tests, linting, and build on every PR; deploys to staging on merge to main

platforms.name: Vercel
platforms.purpose: Frontend hosting with automatic preview deployments per PR branch; production deployment from main

integrations.name: SendGrid
integrations.purpose: Transactional email for password reset and notification delivery
integrations.auth: API key stored in environment variable SENDGRID_API_KEY

integrations.name: Sentry
integrations.purpose: Error tracking and performance monitoring for both frontend and backend
integrations.interface: SDK initialization in app entry points
integrations.auth: DSN stored in environment variable SENTRY_DSN

perception.description: Recipes read intent.yaml to understand goals, constraints, and failure conditions before delegating to agents
repo-orchestrator reads git status, diff, and log to understand current branch state before any commit or PR action

cognition.description: tech-designer decomposes high-level feature intents into step-by-step execution plans with file-level specificity
quality-validator compares actual outputs against expected outcomes in scenario definitions and flags deviations

action.description: code-builder writes, edits, and deletes files per execution plan using Read/Edit/Write/Bash tools
repo-orchestrator stages files, creates commits, manages branches, and opens PRs via git and gh CLI

memory.approach: Dual-tier file-system memory: STM for active work, LTM for persistent knowledge
memory.stm: .meridian/project/{slug}/ — per-session artifacts including intent files, execution plans, and evidence
memory.ltm: ~/.meridian/core/memory/ — global standards, templates, agent definitions, and accumulated knowledge
memory.embeddings: Not implemented in MVP — full-text search over LTM deferred to post-launch based on scale needs

decisions_summary: A Claude Code agent framework using file-system STM/LTM, PostgreSQL + Prisma for structured data, React 18 on Vercel, Tether/Vanish/Orbit approval gates, and GitHub Actions CI/CD — built for single-user CLI usage with full decision auditability.

decisions.question: Should agents communicate via API calls or file-system artifacts?
decisions.answer: File-system artifacts — STM for active work, LTM for persistent knowledge

reasons.text: Single-user CLI tool (PP-1=1) — no network overhead needed, file I/O is local and fast
agent-memory.md: 'File-based STM/LTM provides full auditability — every decision is a file you can inspect'
Explicit state requirement (NFR-1=3) — all decisions must be durable before execution

rejected.reason: unnecessary network overhead for single-user CLI
LLM context windows are ephemeral — state must persist to disk

decisions.question: What database should back the structured data layer?
decisions.answer: PostgreSQL with Prisma ORM for type-safe queries and migration management

reasons.text: backend-nodejs.md: 'Prisma provides type-safe queries and migration management for TypeScript projects'
Small team — Prisma's auto-generated types reduce integration bugs without manual schema maintenance
databases.md: 'PostgreSQL for structured relational data with complex queries'

rejected.reason: relational model fits structured user and settings data better
loses type safety guarantees across frontend-backend boundary

decisions.question: How should recipes enforce human oversight at system boundaries?
decisions.answer: Tether/Vanish/Orbit gate at every boundary crossing — commits, PRs, external APIs

reasons.text: Principle TP3: 'Every action that crosses a system boundary requires explicit human approval'
Business-grade security (NFR-2=3) — autonomous agents at boundaries create irreversible changes
Chosen over auto-approve because cost of one mistake exceeds benefit of many saved approvals

rejected.reason: one irreversible mistake (force push, wrong PR) costs more than approval overhead
breaks agent pipeline flow — clipboard export is non-blocking

technical_risks.risk: LLM non-determinism causes recipe outputs to vary across runs
technical_risks.mitigation: Recipe evals with semantic diff scoring; structured output schemas constrain variation surface area

technical_risks.risk: Prisma migration failures on production database during deployment
technical_risks.mitigation: All migrations run in transactions with automatic rollback; staging environment runs migration before production

deployment.process: GitHub Actions runs tests → builds Docker image → deploys to Vercel preview → runs smoke tests against staging URL

deployment.trigger: Manual promotion via GitHub release tag
deployment.process: Requires explicit approval gate in GitHub Actions → deploys to Vercel production → Sentry release created for error tracking

observability.tracing: Sentry distributed tracing with correlation IDs propagated from frontend requests through API to database queries

metrics.alert_threshold: >1% error rate on any endpoint triggers immediate alert

observability.logging: Structured JSON logging via pino — all log entries include correlation_id, user_id (hashed), endpoint, and duration_ms
